title: Shared resources should be avoided for locking
description: If the instance representing an exclusively acquired lock is publicly accessible, another thread in another part of the program could accidentally attempt to acquire the same lock. This increases the likelihood of deadlocks. For example, a string should never be used for locking.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Avoid locking on shared resources like strings, 'this', or typeof(). Use a private readonly object instead.

logic.reasoning: Locking on publicly accessible resources (strings, 'this', typeof) increases deadlock risk. Private objects should be used for locking.

should_not_trigger:
private readonly object _lock = new object();
lock (_lock) { }
